segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Man in black shirt, wearing an 'i travel for food' t-shirt, seated at a wooden table in a restaurant with colorful translucent panel walls. He actively eats a large king crab, using clear plastic gloves and chopsticks to dip pieces of crab roe into a small metal bowl of white rice, mixing them together with focused enthusiasm. He vocalizes an appreciative "Mmm" and describes the "contrast of the cool, cold, refreshing crab with the hot rice," exclaiming, "Oh, that's good. Wow, that is extraordinary." The table is laden with Korean dishes, including a green pancake and various side dishes, with a camera placed to the side for vlogging. He gestures expressively while speaking to the camera, introducing "another version of the crabs, which is called Yang Yong Gyejang," explaining that these are "raw crabs, this time it's marinated in more of a pepper paste." The scene cuts to a close-up of a spicy, red-sauced crab dish topped with sesame seeds, highlighting its vibrant texture. The camera returns to the man, who continues to eat and interact with the food, occasionally using a spoon to scoop roe from the crab shell, reacting with an "Oh, wow" and later describing the dish as "amazing," "melting your mouth," and having a "stronger flavor with that pepper paste with the sesame seeds." The Woman in striped shirt, also eating with chopsticks and occasionally using her phone, briefly interjects with "Okay, I understand" in Korean. A close-up then shows a different crab dish with a golden-brown sauce and visible roe, being scooped with chopsticks and a spoon. The man explains, "This is the best part, and how we eat the head with all of the roe and the juice in there." The segment concludes with a close-up of the man’s hands using chopsticks and a spoon to extract rich, creamy crab roe from a shell, as he states, "If if I go with my friends, we never ever surrender." Throughout the segment, sounds of eating and background chatter are audible.

He further praises a dish as "amazing," "melting your mouth," and having a "stronger flavor with that pepper paste with the sesame seeds," before detailing how "the best part" is eating "the head with all of the roe and the juice." He concludes with a spirited "If if I go with my friends, we never ever surrender."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a white bowl holding a cracked king crab shell, revealing its golden-orange roe and translucent white meat. A hand uses a metal spoon and chopsticks to carefully scoop out the glistening, gelatinous roe, with a voice (Man in yellow shirt) describing the action as 'digging deep, deep, deep inside' to 'get everything out from the corner.' The roe is then mixed with white rice from a small metal bowl, creating a vibrant orange-and-white mixture, as the voice instructs to put 'rice into the shell' in a 'bibimbap style.' The camera pulls back to reveal a dining table laden with various Korean dishes, including kimchi, pickled vegetables, and a stone pot soup, confirming the central dish is 'soy crab' or 'Ganjang Gejang.' The Man in yellow shirt, wearing an 'i travel for food' shirt, is seated at the table, actively eating the crab and rice mixture with chopsticks and a spoon, smiling and speaking to the camera. He enthusiastically declares this is 'the best part and best way to enjoy the soy crab,' specifically 'Ganjang Gejang,' humorously adding that 'to this moment, there's no friend, there's no wife, there's no family,' just the complete focus on the food. Beside him, the Man in black shirt, also wearing an 'i travel for food' shirt, is eating with chopsticks, occasionally interjecting with an 'Mhm' and laughter, and confirming 'Best part of Ganjang Gejang' and 'That is when you eat Ganjang Gejang.' The restaurant setting features wooden tables, large windows with blinds, and a warm, casual atmosphere, with background chatter and ambient sounds. The men appear engaged and enjoying their food, capturing a lively, authentic Korean dining experience centered around the luxurious king crab.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a lively dining scene at a Korean restaurant, where the Man in yellow shirt, wearing an 'i travel for food' t-shirt, is seated at a table laden with various dishes, including a large plate of king crab, side dishes like kimchi, and bowls of rice. He actively eats with chopsticks and a spoon, engaging with the camera, humorously declaring, "no more friends, every man for himself," a sentiment the Man in black shirt, also wearing an 'i travel for food' t-shirt and eating with a spoon, agrees with. The Man in yellow shirt then enthusiastically describes extracting "the greatest delicacy of the head" from the crab, noting the "pepper paste" within, before instructing to mix "rice goes in, just mix it up," accompanied by distinct eating and slurping sounds. He exclaims "Wow" and praises the "absolutely phenomenal dinner," identifying it as "Ganjang Gejang, raw soy-marinated crabs," which he highly recommends. The camera then zooms in on a close-up of the spicy crab dish, showing the rich red sauce being mixed with the crab meat and rice, emphasizing its texture and vibrant color. The Man in yellow shirt concludes by stating, "Tomorrow, we go to the airport." The scene transitions to the Man in black shirt at a different table, enjoying a bowl of spicy crab over rice, with a camera and other dining items on the table. The video then cuts to an exterior night shot of a restaurant named 'Pro Soy Crab' in Seoul, with a wet street and a white van parked outside, indicating a rainy evening, as background music begins to play. The scene shifts to the Man in black shirt inside a hotel room, speaking to the camera with a smile, pointing at the viewer. The video then shows a nighttime cityscape with the 'IPARK' building illuminated, followed by a transition to 'DAY 11: INCHEON' text over a dark room. The camera pans to a bright morning view from a hotel window, revealing a panoramic cityscape of Incheon with modern buildings, a river, and distant mountains under a partly cloudy sky. The final shot is a close-up of the Man in black shirt lying in bed, smiling at the camera, indicating a relaxed start to the day, all set to the continuing background music.

The video opens with a wide shot of the modern, spacious Incheon International Airport arrival hall, showcasing a large digital flight board, travelers, and signage in Korean and English, accompanied by the ambient sounds of an airport. The scene transitions to the exterior where a man in a black t-shirt with 'i travel for food' printed on it, identified as Mark Wiens, walks with a suitcase and exchanges "Kamsahamnida" and "Thank you" with a man in a white t-shirt featuring a kangaroo logo, before stating, "Here we are, back at Incheon Airport, where it all began." As he walks through the airport, passing a woman in a striped shirt, upbeat travel music begins. The scene shifts to Mark, now in a blue 'i travel for food' t-shirt, seated on a teal bench, gesturing animatedly as he speaks directly to the camera. He expresses his deep appreciation for South Korea's people, culture, regions, and "unforgettable memories of South Korean food." He thanks viewers for watching his "Korean food documentary," encourages them to "give this video a thumbs up" and "share it with a friend who also loves to travel and eat," and extends "a huge thank you to the Korea Tourism Organization, KTO, for making this entire trip happen," concluding with the advice, "when you're in South Korea, try it all." A graphic overlay displays his social media handle '@MarkWiens' and a 'Subscribed' button, as he continues to smile and talk with the airport terminal visible behind him.
